name: Spicy Pork with Peas
recipeIngredient: 3 cups fresh or canned chicken stock or broth
2 lbs pork, sliced and cut into thin strips
5 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
2 cups frozen green peas, thawed or fresh uncooked
1 can plum tomatoes or 1 cup fresh tomatoes, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, mix together the red pepper flakes, Szechuan peppercorn, cornstarch (if using), chicken stock, sesame oil, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar. Set aside.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat until very hot.
3. Add the pork to the pan and cook quickly, tossing until brown all over, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove the pork to a platter.
4. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil to the pan. Then add the onion, garlic, ginger, and carrots. Cook quickly, stirring until crisp tender, another 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Return the pork to the pan and stir in the seasoning mixture until the sauce begins to boil and thicken.
6. Add the peas and cook for about 1 minute more, until heated through.
7. Serve over steaming rice. Sprinkle with parsley and chopped tomatoes, if desired.
8. Serves 4
